_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in Pre-A1/002 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Anna; computer plays Tom. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 002 · Run and Jump

routes.meaning: Anna sees the ball and asks Tom if he can run.
examples: I see it. Can you run?
I see the ball. Can you run?
Yes, I see it! Can you run?
I see it! Can you run fast?
routes.hint: Say what you see and ask Tom: “I see it. Can you run?”

routes.meaning: Anna asks where the ball is.
routes.hint: You can say “I see it. Can you run?” or ask “Where is the ball?”

partial.message: Tell Tom you see it: “I see it. Can you run?”

off_topic.message: Tom found the ball! Say “I see it. Can you run?”

npc.text: It is right here! Now, can you see it?

routes.meaning: Anna sees the ball now and asks if Tom can run.
examples: I see it. Can you run?
Yes, I see it! Can you run?
Now I see it. Can you run?
routes.hint: Say “I see it. Can you run?”

partial.message: The ball is here! Say “I see it. Can you run?”

off_topic.message: Look at the ball. Try “I see it. Can you run?”

routes.meaning: Anna tells Tom to run to the big tree.
routes.hint: Tell Tom where to run: “Run to the big tree!”

partial.message: Tell Tom where to run: “Run to the big tree!”

off_topic.message: Tom can run fast! Say “Run to the big tree!”

npc.text: I can do it. Look at me!

routes.meaning: Anna says Tom is fast and tells him to jump up.
examples: You are fast! Now jump up.
You are fast! Can you jump up?
routes.hint: Tell Tom he is fast, then say jump: “You are fast! Now jump up.”

routes.meaning: Anna only says Tom is fast.
routes.hint: Nice! Now tell Tom to jump: “You are fast! Now jump up.”

partial.message: Tell Tom to jump: “You are fast! Now jump up.”

off_topic.message: Tom ran to the tree! Say “You are fast! Now jump up.”

npc.text: I can jump. Can you jump too?

routes.meaning: Anna says she can jump up and down.
examples: Yes, I can jump up and down.
Yes! I can jump up and down.
I can jump up and down, too.
routes.hint: Say you can jump: “Yes, I can jump up and down.”

routes.meaning: Anna is not sure she can jump.
routes.hint: You can try! Say “Yes, I can jump up and down.”

partial.message: Can you jump? Say “Yes, I can jump up and down.”

off_topic.message: Tom asked if you can jump. Try “Yes, I can jump up and down.”

npc.text: Come on, you can do it! Can you jump up?

examples: Yes, I can jump up and down.
Okay, I can jump up and down.
routes.hint: Say “Yes, I can jump up and down.”

partial.message: You can do it! Say “Yes, I can jump up and down.”

off_topic.message: Give it a try! Say “Yes, I can jump up and down.”

npc.text: That is fun! Let us play.

routes.meaning: Anna says she likes to play with Tom.
examples: Good! I like to play with you.
Yes! I like to play with you.
I like to play with you!
routes.hint: Say you like to play: “Good! I like to play with you.”

partial.message: Say you like to play: “Good! I like to play with you.”

off_topic.message: Tom wants to play. Try “Good! I like to play with you.”

npc.text: Yay! Let us run and jump together. This is fun!
